drill_or_video_name: Elevated Trail Leg Drill (Lead Leg Block Progression)
equipment: plyo ball, mound or flat ground
description: Trail leg elevated behind on a box so the athlete cannot push off; forces the front side to post and feel the natural sequencing of a lead-leg block instead of leaking back-hip energy. Front leg learns to brace and extend in response to pelvic rotation.
notes: Tread Lead Leg Block progression series — entry drill. relevance: directly trains the lead-leg block / front-side brace Landon lacks; removes back-hip push so he can't leak.

drill_or_video_name: Fixing Your Lead Leg Block: Hip Rotation w/ Strap
description: Strap anchored to drive hip rotation into a braced front leg; teaches the back hip to clear/rotate AROUND a posted front leg rather than drifting open. The front leg blocks and extends as the pelvis rotates through.
notes: Tread Lead Leg Block series — strap-constraint variation. relevance: fixes the leaking back hip + open front-leg landing; trains rotate-into-block, Landon's #1/#2 seam.

drill_or_video_name: Problem Solving Your Front Leg Mechanics
description: Breaks down front-leg bracing as a commonly misunderstood concept — the lead leg straightens in RESPONSE to the back hip continuing to rotate around the front hip, not by actively pushing. Diagnostic for early front-side fly-open and a soft (non-blocking) front leg.
notes: relevance: diagnoses Landon's exact fault — lead leg lands open / no brace, so the block never decelerates the pelvis to whip the arm.

drill_or_video_name: Throw Harder by Mastering the Lead Leg Block [Full Routine]
description: Full lead-leg-block routine: progresses from constraint pattern work into loaded throws emphasizing high ground reaction force and front-knee extension at max external rotation. Front leg posts to decelerate the pelvis and redirect energy up the chain.
notes: Tread Lead Leg Block 'Full Routine' series. relevance: the headline fix for Landon — converts hip drive into a braced front side that whips, not pushes, the arm.

drill_or_video_name: 6 Drills To Help Fix Your Front Leg
description: Six-drill front-leg sequence (posting, anti-collapse, brace-and-extend variations) to stop the lead leg from collapsing or landing open; builds a stable front side to rotate around.
notes: Tread front-leg drill compilation. relevance: multiple variations to fix lead-leg-block / front-side collapse — Landon's single most important feature.

drill_or_video_name: "Using Your Legs" — Ground Reaction Forces and Energy Flow
description: Explains how a good lead leg block produces high ground reaction forces and redirects back-leg momentum up the chain to torso and arm; frames why a leaking back hip + soft front leg bleeds velocity. Conceptual primer for the block.
notes: relevance: explains why Landon's leaked back hip + open front leg = no energy transfer to the arm (push, not whip).

drill_or_video_name: Lead Hip Flexion (hamstring loaded)
description: Weight-room lead-leg-block builder: loaded hip flexion biased to the hamstring to strengthen the front-hip's ability to absorb and post against landing forces.
notes: Tread 'lead leg block exercises in the weight room' list (1/7). relevance: builds front-hip capacity to brace — prerequisite for the lead-leg block Landon can't currently hold.

drill_or_video_name: Lead Hip Internal Rotation
description: Loaded lead-hip IR so the front hip can pull back/rotate around a posted leg and the pelvis can clear without the front side flying open.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (2/7). relevance: front-hip IR lets the lead leg block instead of landing open — directly targets Landon's early fly-open.

drill_or_video_name: Hip Airplanes
description: Single-leg hip airplane to train controlled hip IR/ER and single-leg stability — the front-leg posting/balance demand of a lead-leg block.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (3/7). relevance: trains the front leg to post and rotate around without collapsing — the brace Landon lacks.

drill_or_video_name: Rotational KB Swing
description: Rotational kettlebell swing to train ballistic transverse-plane hip drive and clearing — back-hip rotation power feeding into a braced front side.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (4/7). relevance: trains back-hip rotation/clearing power so the hip drives the block rather than leaking.

drill_or_video_name: Split-Stance MB Taps
description: Split-stance med-ball taps in a stride-like position to train front-leg bracing and rhythmic rotation off a stable front side.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (5/7). relevance: stride-specific bracing rep — front leg posts while trunk rotates, mirroring the block Landon needs.

drill_or_video_name: Ipsilateral Cable Row
description: Same-side cable row to train connection between the front side and trunk — reinforces holding the front side/trunk closed against rotational pull.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (6/7). relevance: front-side connection so the trunk stays neutral/closed while the hips rotate (separation).

drill_or_video_name: 3D Strap Rotations
description: Multi-plane strap rotations driving hip-led rotation through the front-leg block in all three planes; patterns rotate-into-brace.
notes: Tread weight-room lead-leg-block list (7/7). relevance: rehearses hip rotation INTO a posted front leg — the rotate-then-block sequence Landon misses.

drill_or_video_name: Throw Harder By Mastering Hip-Shoulder Separation [Full Routine]
description: Full separation routine: counter-rotation and band-tension constraints to feel the pelvis rotating underneath a neutral, closed trunk, progressing into loaded throws. Trunk stays closed while hips fire.
notes: Tread Hip-Shoulder Separation 'Full Routine' series. relevance: restores the separation window Landon loses when his shoulders fly open with the hips.

drill_or_video_name: 7 Drills To Increase Hip To Shoulder Separation
description: Seven constraint drills to grow hip-shoulder separation: pelvis-underneath-neutral-trunk and counter-rotation variations; keep the front shoulder closed while the pelvis rotates.
notes: Tread separation series. relevance: trains trunk-neutral-while-hips-rotate — exactly the separation Landon can't create.

drill_or_video_name: Throw Harder By Using Hip Shoulder Separation
description: Explainer + drill on how separation is created and timed (positive separation at foot strike); diagnoses shoulders flying open early with the pelvis.
notes: relevance: identifies Landon's lost separation (shoulders open with hips) and the late-trunk fix.

drill_or_video_name: How To Feel Hip-Shoulder Separation (band + counter-rotation constraints)
description: Two stacked constraints for pitchers who can't feel pelvis/trunk independence: (1) band tension to open the back hip, (2) a counter-rotation component. Cue: relax, don't force the hips through.
notes: relevance: back-hip band tension + counter-rotation are the exact constraints for Landon's leaked back hip + lost separation.

drill_or_video_name: Hip-Shoulder Separation Explained in 90 Seconds
description: Defines separation as the pelvis and trunk separating: created by the pelvis rotating under a neutral trunk, the trunk counter-rotating, or both. Conceptual diagnostic.
notes: relevance: gives Landon/coach the model for what separation should feel like before drilling it.

drill_or_video_name: Pivot Pickoff Throw
description: Pivot-pickoff constraint throw that loads counter-rotation/coil and teaches elite hip-shoulder separation by forcing the pelvis to lead while the trunk stays back.
notes: Tread Pivot Pickoff modifications/variations. relevance: builds the coil + separation Landon never opens; keeps trunk closed as the pelvis fires.

drill_or_video_name: Pivot Pickoff Modifications and Variations
description: Walks through modifications of the pivot pickoff to scale the separation/coil demand and clean up sequencing into landing.
notes: Tread Pivot Pickoff series. relevance: scalable separation/coil work targeting Landon's no-coil, early-open pattern.

drill_or_video_name: The Walking Windup Drill
description: Walking windup that chains the back-chaining drill work into a more sport-specific moving delivery; rhythms the back-hip load and transition into the block.
notes: Tread Walking Windup series — transition drill. relevance: bridges the drop->rotate transition Landon fails, keeping flow into a posted front side.

drill_or_video_name: Gain Velocity by Fixing Your Glove Arm (3 Glove Arm Drills)
description: Three glove-arm drills to stay closed: control the glove and pull the lead elbow down to the side as you rotate, holding the front side a beat longer rather than flying the glove open.
notes: relevance: glove-side discipline keeps the front side closed — directly attacks Landon's shoulders flying open early.

drill_or_video_name: How to Feel the ""Hinge"" (Pitching Drills)
description: Teaches the back-hip hinge/load so the pelvis can rotate from a hinged, loaded position rather than lunging or pushing off the back leg.
notes: relevance: builds the loaded back-hip position Landon gets (good drop) and teaches converting it to rotation, not a leak.

drill_or_video_name: Mastering the Rocker Drill
description: Rocker drill to teach proper weight shift and center-of-mass control — load back, then sequence the back leg into the move rather than drifting.
notes: relevance: cleans up the drift/weight-shift so Landon's back hip loads to rotate instead of leaking forward early.

drill_or_video_name: The ""Drift"" — How to Shift Your Weight Like a Pro
description: Article on shifting weight into the back hip correctly so the lower half can sequence; addresses drifting too early and losing the load.
notes: relevance: fixes early drift/leak that opens Landon's front side before the separation window.

drill_or_video_name: TRX Drift Drill (Struggling to Feel Your Lower Half)
description: TRX-supported drift drill to feel loading into the back hip and sequencing the lower half without falling forward; isolates the back-leg load.
notes: relevance: TRX constraint helps Landon feel the back-hip load before rotation, reducing the early leak.

drill_or_video_name: Drive Leg Mechanics to Increase Velocity & Reduce Arm Stress
description: Breaks down rear-glute/hamstring drive (pull the ground back like a sprinter) vs quad push; back-hip drive that reduces arm stress.
notes: relevance: trains back-hip DRIVE (glute/ham) to stop the leak; lower arm stress matters for Landon's flexor-pronator history.

drill_or_video_name: Problem Solving Your Lower Half Pitching Mechanics
description: Cornerstone lower-half article: rear-glute drive, step-back drills, rotate vs lunge off the back leg, and front-leg brace in response to hip rotation.
notes: relevance: covers Landon's whole lower-half chain — back-hip drive, rotate-not-lunge, and the lead-leg brace.

drill_or_video_name: Re-thinking How the Back Hip Works Into Landing
description: Reframes the unload: the pelvis (middle of the body) drives rotation into landing, not the rear femur slamming into IR. Better mental model for clearing the back hip.
notes: relevance: corrects how Landon should think about the back-hip unload so it rotates/clears instead of leaking.

drill_or_video_name: Quick Pick Drill
description: Quick-pick constraint drill highlighted to clean up sequencing and timing into a closed front side; fast rep to groove rotate-late pattern.
notes: Tread drill highlight. relevance: grooves late rotation/closed sequencing — counters Landon's early front-hip swing.

drill_or_video_name: How to Fix a Pushing Arm Action — 7 Causes and Solutions
description: Diagnoses 7 causes of a pushing arm action (too-heavy balls, fake layback via elbow flexion, lost hip-shoulder separation, tight lats/triceps, injury compensation) and the fixes — keep shoulders closed at landing so the arm stays in line and whips.
notes: relevance: directly addresses Landon's PUSH arm action; note the injury-compensation cause given his flexor-pronator history — keep ball weights <=6oz.

drill_or_video_name: How to Sync the Arm into the Plane of Shoulder Rotation
description: Teaches keeping the arm IN the plane of shoulder rotation instead of finishing straight down when the torso flexes to 'throw strikes'; lets the trunk rotate the arm through in plane (spiral, not push).
notes: relevance: converts Landon's push/out-of-plane finish into an in-plane spiral driven by trunk rotation.

drill_or_video_name: The 3 Types of ""Muscling Up"" — How It Hurts Your Velocity
description: Explains three ways pitchers muscle up the arm (tense/push) instead of letting the trunk reflexively whip the arm into release; cue to relax the arm and let pecs/lats fire reflexively.
notes: relevance: names the tense/push habit Landon defaults to without a braced front side; primes the whip feel.

drill_or_video_name: 3 Drills To Throw Harder By Transferring Energy
description: Three transfer-of-energy drills chaining ground force -> hip rotation -> braced front leg -> trunk -> arm; loaded but sub-max constraint progression into throwing.
notes: relevance: trains the full energy transfer Landon loses; keep intent sub-max + balls <=6oz given conservative-load year.

drill_or_video_name: Are Rotational Medicine Ball Throws the Key to Pitching Velocity?
description: Rotational med-ball throws (scoop/shotput/side) that correlate with throwing velocity; transverse-plane rotational power for hip-led sequencing.
notes: relevance: builds rotational CNS/power for hip-shoulder separation; underload/med-ball is arm-stress-safe for Landon's elbow history.

drill_or_video_name: How Does Hip Anatomy Affect Your Ideal Mechanics?
description: Explains how individual hip anatomy (femoral version, range) shapes ideal stance/rotation, so cueing 'stay closed' and hip rotation is individualized rather than one-size-fits-all.
notes: relevance: ensures Landon's stay-closed/hip-rotation targets are matched to his hip anatomy before loading the block.
